title: Cohere releases North Mini Code, an open model for agentic coding
friendlyTitle: Cohere releases an open coding model for agent workflows
summary: Cohere released North Mini Code, a 30B-parameter sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 3B active parameters, available under Apache 2.0. The model is built for agentic coding, terminal tasks, complex code generation, and long-context software workflows across multiple agent scaffolds.
friendlySummary: North Mini Code is an open model built for coding agents, terminal work, and software engineering tasks. It is available through Hugging Face, OpenCode, and Cohere’s API.
whyItMatters: Coding models are increasingly optimized for full agent loops rather than only code completion. Open coding models give teams more options for private, inspectable, and cost-controlled developer workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Developers get another open option for coding assistants. That matters for teams that want more control over privacy, cost, and how their coding tools behave.
